hero.headline: West Bengal's GDP Growth Rate Hits 7.4% in Q4: Report
hero.subheadline: Economic growth in the state surpasses national average, driven by manufacturing and services sectors
hero.summary: The GDP growth rate of West Bengal accelerated to 7.4% in the fourth quarter of the 2025-26 fiscal year, surpassing the national average, according to a report by the Reserve Bank of India. This growth was largely driven by the manufacturing and services sectors, which contributed significantly to the state's economic expansion. The report also noted that the growth rate was higher than the previous quarter's 6.8%. The West Bengal government has attributed this growth to its policies aimed at promoting industry and entrepreneurship in the state. The growth is expected to boost the state's economy and create more job opportunities for its citizens.
hero.impact: Strong economic growth in West Bengal expected to create jobs and boost state's economy

stories.headline: Farmers in West Bengal to Benefit from New Crop Insurance Scheme
stories.summary: The West Bengal government has launched a new crop insurance scheme to provide financial protection to farmers in case of crop failure or damage. The scheme, which was launched in collaboration with the National Insurance Company, will provide coverage to farmers for their crops against natural calamities such as floods, droughts, and cyclones. The scheme will also provide financial assistance to farmers for crop damage caused by pests and diseases. The government has set a target to cover 50% of the state's farmers under the scheme within the next six months.

stories.headline: New Healthcare Facility to be Launched in West Bengal's South 24 Parganas
stories.summary: A new healthcare facility is set to be launched in South 24 Parganas district of West Bengal. The facility, which will be a 100-bed hospital, will provide medical services to the residents of the district. The hospital will have state-of-the-art equipment and will have a team of experienced doctors and paramedics. The facility is expected to improve healthcare services in the district and reduce the burden on existing hospitals. The West Bengal government has allocated Rs. 50 crores for the construction of the hospital.

stories.headline: West Bengal Government Announces Plans to Upgrade State's Road Network
stories.summary: The West Bengal government has announced plans to upgrade the state's road network. The government has allocated Rs. 1000 crores for the upgrading of roads in the state. The project will include the construction of new roads, widening of existing roads, and improvement of road safety. The government has also announced plans to introduce a new public transportation system in the state. The project is expected to improve connectivity and reduce travel time in the state.

stories.headline: [UNVERIFIED] West Bengal to Launch New Education Policy
stories.summary: [UNVERIFIED] The West Bengal government is set to launch a new education policy. The policy is expected to focus on improving the quality of education in the state. [UNVERIFIED] The government has announced plans to introduce new curriculum and teaching methods. [UNVERIFIED] The policy is expected to improve the education sector in the state.

stories.topic: Migrant worker deaths in West Bengal

state_narrative.headline: West Bengal govt. claims low number of migrant worker deaths during lockdown
state_narrative.summary: According to a report by ABP Ananda, the West Bengal government stated that only 22 migrant workers had died in the state during the COVID-19 lockdown. The report attributed the low number of deaths to the state's effective lockdown management and migrant worker support mechanisms.
claims_made: Only 22 migrant worker deaths during lockdown
Effective lockdown management and migrant worker support mechanisms

verified_record.headline: Migrant worker deaths in West Bengal: A closer look at the data
verified_record.summary: According to FactChecker.in, citing data from the National Crime Records Bureau (NCRB), at least 75 migrant workers died in West Bengal during the lockdown. The article highlights that the state's actual number of migrant worker deaths was significantly higher than the reported figure.
what_was_omitted: Migrant worker deaths due to accidents and other causes
State's actual number of migrant worker deaths
verified_record.data_check: Source: NCRB data on accidental deaths and cases under IPC (Section 302)

gap_analysis.key_gap: The state government's reported figure of 22 migrant worker deaths was a significant undercount compared to the actual number of deaths.
gap_analysis.missing_context: The state's lockdown management and migrant worker support mechanisms, while commendable, did not account for the actual number of migrant worker deaths in the state.

state_narrative.headline: West Bengal govt. claims farmers' protests are a ploy by opposition parties
state_narrative.summary: According to a report by Zee News, the West Bengal government stated that the farmers' protests in the state were a ploy by opposition parties to destabilize the government. The report cited alleged plans by opposition parties to disrupt the state's agriculture sector.
claims_made: Farmers' protests are a ploy by opposition parties
Opposition parties' alleged plans to disrupt the state's agriculture sector

verified_record.headline: West Bengal farmers' protests: A look at the demands and concerns
verified_record.summary: According to The Wire, farmers in West Bengal have been protesting against the state government's policies and lack of support for the agriculture sector. The article highlights that the farmers' demands include better prices for their produce and more support for rural infrastructure.
what_was_omitted: Farmers' demands and concerns regarding government policies
Lack of support for the agriculture sector
verified_record.data_check: Source: Farmers' organizations and local reports

gap_analysis.key_gap: The state government's narrative on farmers' protests was partially accurate, but omitted the farmers' legitimate demands and concerns.
gap_analysis.missing_context: The state government's policies and lack of support for the agriculture sector have been major concerns for farmers in West Bengal.

state_narrative.headline: West Bengal govt. claims efforts to protect Sundarbans
state_narrative.summary: According to a report by News18 Bangla, the West Bengal government stated that it was making efforts to protect the Sundarbans mangrove forest from environmental degradation. The report cited initiatives such as afforestation programs and conservation efforts.
claims_made: Initiatives such as afforestation programs and conservation efforts

verified_record.headline: Sundarbans in crisis: A look at the environmental degradation
verified_record.summary: According to Scroll.in, the Sundarbans mangrove forest is facing severe environmental degradation due to human activities such as deforestation and pollution. The article highlights that the state government's initiatives have been insufficient to address the crisis.
what_was_omitted: Severe environmental degradation due to human activities
verified_record.data_check: Source: Government reports and environmental studies

gap_analysis.key_gap: The state government's narrative on efforts to protect Sundarbans was misleading, as it omitted the severity of environmental degradation and the insufficiency of its initiatives.
gap_analysis.missing_context: The state government's initiatives, while commendable, have been insufficient to address the severe environmental degradation in Sundarbans.

hottake.headline: Ganges River Water Quality Hits Record Low in June 2026
bullets: Water samples from 13 locations along the Ganges showed 50% more bacteria than last year.
The Ganges' water quality has deteriorated by 20% since 2020, according to the latest data from the Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB).
This means people in Kolkata and other West Bengal cities may face increased risk of waterborne diseases like cholera and typhoid.

hottake.headline: West Bengal Food Prices Rise 15% in Past 6 Months
bullets: Data from the Department of Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution shows a significant increase in prices of essential commodities.
Rice prices have risen by 12%, while dal prices have surged by 20% in the same period.
This means West Bengal residents may struggle to afford basic necessities like food and groceries.
hottake.source: Department of Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution

hottake.headline: West Bengal Government Announces Free Education for All
bullets: The state government has approved a budget of ₹500 crore to implement the scheme.
The scheme will benefit over 1 million students from Class 1 to 12th standard.
This means thousands of students from low-income families can now access quality education for free.

hottake.headline: Thousands of Workers Stage Protest Against Minimum Wage Hike Delay
bullets: Workers from various industries, including construction and manufacturing, are demanding a minimum wage hike of ₹20,000.
According to data from the Confederation of Indian Trade Unions (CITU), over 50,000 workers participated in the protest.
This means workers in West Bengal may face difficulties in making ends meet if the minimum wage hike is delayed.

hottake.headline: Newborn Mortality Rate in West Bengal Rises 12% in 2026
bullets: Data from the National Health Mission shows a significant increase in newborn deaths in the first 28 days of life.
According to the data, over 10,000 newborns died in West Bengal in the first 6 months of 2026.
This means pregnant women and new mothers in West Bengal may face increased risks during childbirth.

hottake.headline: West Bengal Government Announces New Metro Line Project
bullets: The new metro line will connect Howrah to the proposed new town of Naihati.
The project is expected to cost ₹5,000 crore and will create over 10,000 jobs during construction.
This means commuters in Howrah and surrounding areas may face reduced travel times and improved connectivity.

faq.question: How do I apply for Swasthya Sathi?
faq.answer:
**Swasthya Sathi** is West Bengal's universal health coverage scheme providing ₹5 lakh annual coverage per family.

**Eligibility:** All families in West Bengal with a valid ration card. The card is issued in the name of the woman head of household.

**How to apply:**
1. Visit your nearest **Duare Sarkar** camp (held periodically in every block/ward)
2. Or apply online at **swasthyasathi.gov.in**
3. Bring: Ration card, Aadhaar card, one passport photo
4. The smart card is issued on the spot at camps, or mailed within 15 days for online applications

**What's covered:** Hospitalization, surgeries, diagnostics, medicines at 1,500+ empanelled hospitals across Bengal.

**Helpline:** 1800-345-5384 (toll-free)

*(Source: Department of Health & Family Welfare, Govt. of West Bengal)*

faq.question: What is West Bengal's unemployment rate?
faq.answer:
According to the latest available data:

- **CMIE (April 2026):** West Bengal's unemployment rate is **6.8%**, marginally below the national average of 7.1%
- **PLFS 2024-25:** The state's labor force participation rate is 52.1% (male: 76.2%, female: 26.3%)
- **Youth unemployment (15-29 years):** Estimated at 18.4%, above the national average of 16.2%

**Key context:** West Bengal's female labor force participation rate (26.3%) is significantly below the national average (32.8%), indicating structural barriers to women's workforce entry.

The state's largest employers are agriculture (42%), services (36%), and manufacturing (22%). The informal sector accounts for approximately 89% of total employment.

*(Sources: CMIE Economic Outlook, Periodic Labour Force Survey 2024-25, MOSPI)*

faq.question: How do I file an RTI?
faq.answer:
**Right to Information (RTI)** allows any Indian citizen to request information from government bodies. Here's how:

**Step 1:** Write a simple application addressed to the **Public Information Officer (PIO)** of the relevant department.

**Step 2:** Include:
- Your name and address
- The specific information you're requesting (be precise)
- Write: "I request this information under the Right to Information Act, 2005"

**Step 3:** Pay ₹10 fee (₹0 for BPL card holders) via:
- Court fee stamp, postal order, or demand draft
- Online: **rtionline.gov.in** for central departments
- For WB state departments: Apply at the department office or via registered post

**Timeline:** PIO must respond within **30 days** (48 hours for life/liberty matters).

**If denied:** File a **First Appeal** to the appellate authority within 30 days. Then escalate to the **WB State Information Commission** at wbic.gov.in.

**Helpline:** WB Information Commission — 033-2214-5563

*(Source: RTI Act 2005, WB State Information Commission)*

faq.question: Explain the jute industry crisis
faq.answer:
West Bengal produces **75% of India's raw jute** and houses most of the country's jute mills, primarily along the Hooghly river belt (Howrah, Hooghly, North 24 Parganas).

**The crisis:**
- **Declining demand:** Synthetic alternatives have captured packaging markets that jute traditionally served
- **Aging infrastructure:** Most mills operate on machinery from the 1950s-70s, leading to low productivity
- **Wage disputes:** Workers earn ₹232/day — among the lowest industrial wages in India. Unions demand ₹350+
- **Mill closures:** 12 mills have closed permanently since 2019, displacing ~35,000 workers
- **Raw material price volatility:** Farmers face price crashes during peak harvest, discouraging cultivation

**Government response:**
- **Jute Packaging Material Act** mandates jute packaging for foodgrains and sugar — this keeps ~60% of mills running
- **Jute-ICARE scheme** (₹1,045 crore) for technology modernization
- **MSP for raw jute:** ₹5,350/quintal (2025-26)

**What workers can do:** Contact the Jute Workers' Helpline at the Labour Commissioner's office: 1800-345-4150.

*(Sources: Ministry of Textiles, IJMA Annual Report, The Wire, Indian Jute Mills Association)*

faq.question: What are my rights as a daily wage worker?
faq.answer:
As a daily wage or informal sector worker in West Bengal, you have these legal protections:

**Minimum Wage:**
- WB Minimum Wages Act sets floor wages by industry. Current rates range from ₹232-415/day depending on sector
- **Construction workers:** ₹356/day (skilled), ₹291/day (unskilled)
- Employers MUST pay at least minimum wage — violation is a criminal offense

**Key Rights:**
1. **Equal pay for equal work** — no gender-based wage discrimination (Article 39(d))
2. **MGNREGA:** 100 days guaranteed employment at ₹237/day. Apply at your Gram Panchayat.
3. **WB Building Workers' Welfare Board:** Register for pension (₹1,000/month), medical aid, children's education support
4. **ESI coverage** if your employer has 10+ workers and your salary is below ₹21,000/month
5. **No wage deduction** without written notice and consent

**If your wages are unpaid:**
1. File a complaint at the **District Labour Commissioner** office
2. Call the WB Labour Department helpline: **1800-345-4150**
3. You can also file a case at the **Labour Court** — no lawyer fees required

**Unions:** You have the constitutional right to form or join a trade union (Article 19(1)(c)).

*(Sources: WB Minimum Wages Act, MGNREGA Act 2005, Building & Other Construction Workers Act 1996)*

faq.question: Tell me about the MGNREGA scheme in WB
faq.answer:
**MGNREGA (Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act)** guarantees 100 days of wage employment per year to every rural household.

**In West Bengal:**
- **Wage rate:** ₹237/day (2025-26)
- **Person-days generated (2023-24):** 41.3 crore — West Bengal is among the top 5 states by utilization
- **Households benefited:** ~1.2 crore annually

**How to apply:**
1. Visit your **Gram Panchayat** office
2. Submit a written application demanding work (keep a receipt!)
3. Bring: Aadhaar card, bank passbook, passport photo
4. You'll receive a **Job Card** — this is your legal entitlement

**Your rights under MGNREGA:**
- Work must be provided **within 15 days** of application, or you're entitled to an unemployment allowance
- Wages must be paid **within 15 days** of work completion, directly to your bank account
- **Worksite facilities** are mandatory: drinking water, shade, first-aid, crèche for children
- **No contractor system** — work must be executed by the Gram Panchayat

**Common issues in WB:**
- Wage payment delays reported in several districts (average: 25-40 days vs mandated 15)
- Some panchayats show 'ghost beneficiaries' — report this via the Grievance Portal

**Grievance redressal:** nrega.nic.in/netnrega → 'Register Grievance' or call 1800-345-22-44

*(Sources: mgnregs.nic.in, Ministry of Rural Development, CAG Performance Audit 2024-25)*
